instruction:
Find a curve $L: y \left(x \right)$such that $y (x)=()$ is continuously differentiable on $(-\infty,+\infty)$such that at every point on the curve $L$the intersection of the tangent line with the horizontal axis is equal to half the abscission of the tangent point.

Options:
A. $y = Cx^{2}+2x$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
B. $y = Cx^{4}$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
C. $y = Cx^{3}$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
D. $y = Cx^{2}-2$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
E. $y = Cx^{2}$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
F. $y = Cx^{}$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
G. $y = C \sin(x)$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
H. $y = Ce^{x}$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
I. $y = Cx^{2}+2$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
J. $y = Cx^{2}+x$, where $C$ is any nonzero constant
